name: Elaine's Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, blend together the graham cracker crumbs, butter or margarine, and 3 tablespoons granulated sugar. Press the mixture into the bottom of a greased 8-inch or 9-inch springform pan. Place the pan in the freezer to chill while preparing the filling.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine the cream cheese and sugar until smooth and light. Mix in the eggs, vanilla, cornstarch, and sour cream until well combined. The mixture should be light yellow in color.
3. Pour the batter into the prepared springform pan.
4. Bake at 450°F (232°C) for 10 minutes. Reduce the temperature to 200°F (93°C) and bake for another 45 minutes. Turn off the oven and let the cheesecake cool in the oven with the door slightly open for 2.5 to 3 hours.
5. Remove the sides of the pan and chill the cheesecake in the refrigerator for an additional 2 to 3 hours. To serve, carefully slide the cheesecake from the bottom of the pan if desired.
You can use Oreo cookie crumbs instead of graham cracker crumbs for a delightful variation. (This is how most people order it from me.)
Sprinkle some crumbs on top as a garnish before placing the cheesecake in the oven to bake.
Place a pan with about 2-3 inches of hot water on the bottom shelf to prevent cracking. Always bake the cheesecake on the middle shelf—no lower!
